OBSERVATORY

Geodesic Data Dome
LIVE Three.js r160WebGL BloomES Modules
⬡ Launch OBSERVATORY ← Back to Hub
What Is It

Full geodesic dome — each triangular panel is a live data node. Geodesic Data Dome is a core component of the P31 Labs sovereign cognitive infrastructure stack — built to run offline, deployed to the edge, and designed without dark patterns.

Geodesic geometry generated procedurally from a frequency-2 icosphere subdivision. Panel data is seeded from the Kenosis mesh state via a periodic fetch to the k4-cage worker.

Core Features
  • Full geodesic dome — each triangular panel is a live data node
  • Click any panel to inspect its axis, state, bus type, and health metrics
  • Search and filter panels by axis (X/Y/Z), state (active/degraded/offline), or bus
  • Bloom pipeline: degraded nodes pulse amber, offline nodes go dark, active glow teal
  • Dust motes and aurora band background — the dome feels inhabited, not inert
How To Use
1Orbit the dome with left-click drag — look for amber panels indicating degraded nodes
2Click any panel to open the inspector — shows the full node data object
3Use the search bar to filter: type "offline" to find all dark panels at once
Architecture Notes
Geodesic geometry generated procedurally from a frequency-2 icosphere subdivision. Panel data is seeded from the Kenosis mesh state via a periodic fetch to the k4-cage worker.
P31 Labs, Inc. (Georgia nonprofit, EIN 42-1888158) builds open tools for cognitive sovereignty, communication clarity, and family coordination. This page is technical documentation, not medical or legal advice. Mission and support: phosphorus31.org.
